# Resource (hush_azure_app_access_privilege)

Manage Azure app access privileges in the Hush Security platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create an Azure app access privilege with an existing app
resource "hush_azure_app_access_privilege" "existing_app" {
  name        = "azure-storage-access"
  description = "Azure storage access privilege"
  app_id      = "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012"
}

# Create an Azure app access privilege with a new app
resource "hush_azure_app_access_privilege" "new_app" {
  name        = "azure-storage-access-new-app"
  description = "Azure storage access privilege with new app"

  app_config {
    display_name = "My New App"

    roles {
      name  = "Storage Blob Data Reader"
      scope = "/subscriptions/sub-id/resourceGroups/rg-name"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the Azure app access privilege

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the Azure app access privilege
- `app_id` (String) The Azure application ID (UUID). Mutually exclusive with app_config.
- `app_config` (Block List, Max: 1) The Azure application configuration. Mutually exclusive with app_id. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--app_config))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the Azure app access privilege
- `type` (String) The type of access privilege

<a id="nestedblock--app_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `app_config`

Required:

- `display_name` (String) The display name of the Azure application

Optional:

- `roles` (Block List) The list of Azure roles (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--app_config--roles))

<a id="nestedblock--app_config--roles"></a>
### Nested Schema for `app_config.roles`

Required:

- `name` (String) The role name
- `scope` (String) The role scope
